terms.definition: A method of packaging applications and their dependencies into standardized units for software development, deployment, and execution.
terms.example: The team deployed the microservices using containerization to ensure consistent environments across development and production.

terms.definition: A set of automated processes that integrate code changes and deliver them to production environments.
terms.example: Our CI/CD pipeline automatically runs tests and deploys the application whenever a developer pushes code to the main branch.

terms.definition: A security model that assumes no user or device is trusted by default, requiring verification for every access request.
terms.example: The company implemented a zero trust architecture to protect sensitive customer data from unauthorized access.

terms.definition: An open-source container orchestration platform for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications.
terms.example: We migrated our legacy monolith to Kubernetes to improve scalability and reduce infrastructure costs.

terms.definition: The practice of managing and provisioning infrastructure through machine-readable definition files rather than manual configuration.
terms.example: Using Infrastructure as Code, we can version control our AWS resources and apply changes consistently across environments.

terms.definition: A security mechanism requiring users to provide two or more verification factors to gain access to a system.
terms.example: The application enforces multi-factor authentication to prevent unauthorized account access even if passwords are compromised.

terms.definition: A cloud execution model where the cloud provider manages the infrastructure, and developers only write and deploy code.
terms.example: We switched to serverless computing for our event-driven services to reduce operational overhead and pay only for actual usage.

terms.definition: A deployment strategy using two identical production environments to enable zero-downtime releases.
terms.example: The blue-green deployment allowed us to release the new version without interrupting service for our users.

terms.definition: A simulated cyberattack against a computer system to evaluate its security controls and identify vulnerabilities.
terms.example: Our security team conducted quarterly penetration testing to proactively identify and remediate potential attack vectors.

terms.definition: An architectural style that structures an application as a collection of loosely coupled, independently deployable services.
terms.example: Refactoring our monolithic application into microservices improved our team's ability to deploy features independently.
